description:
You are a web–developer who just inherited an old code-base that still contains legacy encoded HTML pages.  
Your goal is to create clean UTF-8 versions of every *.html* file that currently lives in  

/home/user/dev_site/pages/  

The legacy pages are a mix of *windows-1252* and *iso-8859-1* encodings and each file still declares its old encoding inside a <meta charset="..."> element.

WHAT YOU MUST DELIVER
1. A **new directory** called  
   /home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8  
   that contains UTF-8 encoded copies of every original *.html* file.  
   • Filenames must stay identical (e.g. *index.html* → *index.html*).  
   • The only HTML modification allowed is updating the meta charset tag so it reads exactly:  
     <meta charset="utf-8"> (all lowercase, no extra attributes, no self-closing slash).  
   • All textual characters (é, ñ, ü, €, etc.) must appear correctly in their UTF-8 form.

2. A **conversion log** located at  
   /home/user/encoding_conversion.log  

   Format requirements (one line per converted file, **pipe-separated** with no leading/trailing spaces):

   ORIGINAL_PATH|NEW_UTF8_PATH|ORIGINAL_ENCODING|CONVERSION_STATUS|NEW_SHA256

   Example (layout only):  
   /home/user/dev_site/pages/index.html|/home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/index.html|windows-1252|OK|d2c7e55c1d14…

   Field definitions:  
   • ORIGINAL_PATH      – absolute path of the source file.  
   • NEW_UTF8_PATH      – absolute path of the UTF-8 file you created.  
   • ORIGINAL_ENCODING  – the legacy encoding you detected (*windows-1252* or *iso-8859-1*).  
   • CONVERSION_STATUS  – literal string “OK”.  
   • NEW_SHA256         – full 64-character lowercase hex digest of the UTF-8 file.

3. Do NOT delete or overwrite the original files/directories.  
4. Only the files found directly inside /home/user/dev_site/pages/ must be processed (no recursion into sub-folders, if any).  
5. Everything has to be reproducible through normal command-line utilities available on a standard Debian/Ubuntu image (e.g. file, iconv, sed, sha256sum, etc.).

HINTS & CONSTRAINTS
• You are limited to roughly 5–10 commands; plan a small script or a short one-liner pipeline.  
• Ensure /home/user/encoding_conversion.log uses UNIX line endings (LF).  
• The automated checker will confirm:  
  – The presence of /home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/.  
  – Every *.html* inside that directory is UTF-8 encoded (the file command must report “UTF-8”).  
  – Each UTF-8 file’s <meta> tag is exactly as specified.  
  – The conversion log exists, contains the correct number of lines, follows the exact pipe format, and shows valid SHA-256 checksums that match each converted file.  
  – CONVERSION_STATUS is “OK” for every entry.

Finish once all checks would pass.
truth:
Initial sandbox state (before the agent begins):

Directory structure:
/home/user/dev_site/pages/
├── index.html
├── about.html
└── contact.html

Encodings & exact byte contents (newlines are LF):

1. /home/user/dev_site/pages/index.html  
   Encoding: windows-1252  
   Bytes (hex sequence):  
   3c 6d 65 74 61 20 63 68 61 72 73 65 74 3d 22 77 69 6e 64 6f 77 73 2d 31 32 35 32 22 3e 0a  
   3c 68 31 3e 43 72 e8 6d 65 20 42 72 fb 6c e9 65 3c 2f 68 31 3e 0a  
   3c 70 3e 50 72 69 63 65 3a 20 31 30 80 3c 2f 70 3e 0a

   (renders as):  
   <meta charset="windows-1252">  
   <h1>Crème Brûlée</h1>  
   <p>Price: 10€</p>

2. /home/user/dev_site/pages/about.html  
   Encoding: iso-8859-1  
   Bytes:  
   3c 6d 65 74 61 20 63 68 61 72 73 65 74 3d 22 69 73 6f 2d 38 38 35 39 2d 31 22 3e 0a  
   3c 70 3e 53 65 f1 6f 72 20 61 f1 6f 20 63 61 f1 f3 6e 3c 2f 70 3e 0a

   (renders as):  
   <meta charset="iso-8859-1">  
   <p>Señor año cañón</p>

3. /home/user/dev_site/pages/contact.html  
   Encoding: windows-1252  
   Bytes:  
   3c 6d 65 74 61 20 63 68 61 72 73 65 74 3d 22 77 69 6e 64 6f 77 73 2d 31 32 35 32 22 3e 0a  
   3c 70 3e 50 68 6f 6e 65 3a 20 2b 34 39 20 30 33 30 20 31 32 33 34 35 36 3c 2f 70 3e 0a  
   3c 70 3e 45 6d 61 69 6c 3a 20 fc 62 65 72 40 65 78 61 6d 70 6c 65 2e 63 6f 6d 3c 2f 70 3e 0a

   (renders as):  
   <meta charset="windows-1252">  
   <p>Phone: +49 030 123456</p>  
   <p>Email: über@example.com</p>

Expected final artefacts created by the agent:

A. UTF-8 files:
/home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/index.html  
/home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/about.html  
/home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/contact.html  

Each file must:
• Be UTF-8 encoded (validated with the `file` command).  
• Contain the same visible text, but the first line must now be:  
  <meta charset="utf-8">

B. Log file:
/home/user/encoding_conversion.log

Expected content (order of lines does not matter, but each of the three lines must match exactly one of the permutations below; SHA256 placeholders shown here must be replaced by the agent’s real digest values):

/home/user/dev_site/pages/index.html|/home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/index.html|windows-1252|OK|<sha256_index>
/home/user/dev_site/pages/about.html|/home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/about.html|iso-8859-1|OK|<sha256_about>
/home/user/dev_site/pages/contact.html|/home/user/dev_site/pages_utf8/contact.html|windows-1252|OK|<sha256_contact>

Where:
<sha256_index>, <sha256_about>, and <sha256_contact> are the 64-char lowercase SHA-256 sums of their respective UTF-8 files’ full contents.

No other files should be modified or created.
name: task_000000_3be816c4
